raw_consumer_statement:
Consumer beware - does XXXX have XXXX banks? Citibank is terrible and I can not emphasize enough the frustration and disappointment I have experienced working with this lower-tier financial institution. I have had my number stolen at least 6 times, resulting in cancellation of the account and reissuing of a card- which is extremely disruptive! Citi couldnt care less and has made no attempt to improve security. 

My most recent headache - I called 2 weeks ago because I noted unauthorized charges of {$9.00} on the XXXX of every month to a vendor named XXXX. Citi cancelled my card number and reissued a new number, which seems to be their answer to any problem. I also initiated disputes for the previous 6 months of unauthorized charges to this same vendor. 

On first glance, this appears to be a subscription to XXXX premium service, for which I do not have an account. I contacted XXXX and they attempted to locate my account using the last 4 of my card number and expiration date, but they XXXX was unable to find it. This now appears to be a fraudulent vendor who had named itself XXXX and charged a monthly fee in an attempt to disguise themselves. 

I called Citi 2 weeks ago and informed them of this situation. I specifically asked on their recorded telephone line to NOT forward the updated card info to recurring vendors. I asked a second time before the call ended and their representative confirmed she had made the necessary transaction in their system to prevent automatic updates to subscriptions. XXXX and behold, I just received another {$9.00} recurring charge from XXXX on my new card number. 

What is going on at Citibank? How inept does this companys operations need to be before CFPB or 2 weeks later I have seen no progress on the disputed charges ( when are they going to wake up and act on that? ) and once again I had to ask them to cancel my number and reissue a new card. I would love to cancel altogether but their manipulative and unfair rewards rules means that I would forfeit several XXXX dollars of accrued rewards if I cancel before XXXX of XXXX. How is that legal?
